# Part 11 — Bringing It All Back {.sdaia-dark background-gradient="linear-gradient(135deg, #1C355E, #00C9A7)"}

## The Master Translation Table {.smaller}

| Concept | Water Analogy | Electronics Reality |
|---------|--------------|---------------------|
| Voltage (V) | Pump pressure | Force pushing electrons |
| Current (I) | Flow rate | Electrons per second (Amperes) |
| Resistance (R) | Narrow pipe | Opposition to electron flow (Ohms) |
| Ohm's Law | P = F × R | V = I × R |
| Open circuit | Cut pipe | No complete path, no current |
| Short circuit | Removed all pipes | Near-zero resistance, huge current |
| LED / Bulb | Friction heating pipe | Resistance → heat → light |
| LED Polarity | One-way check valve | Current only flows one way (+ to −) |
| Series | Pipes in a single line | Components in a single path (Same Current) |
| Parallel | Branching pipes | Components side-by-side (Same Voltage) |
| Motor | Paddle wheel | EM force drives magnets → rotation |
| Switch | Gate valve | Opens / closes the electron path |
| Variable resistor | Valve | Adjustable resistance, adjustable current |
| Transistor | Electron-controlled valve | Small current controls large current |
| Binary | Open / closed valve | HIGH / LOW voltage states |
| C++ | Instructions to the processor | High-level language the compiler translates |

## 💡 Curiosity Challenges *(Optional — try at home!)* {background-color="#1C355E" .sdaia-dark}

These are **not homework**. Try them if you're curious — there's no deadline and no grade.

::: {.incremental}

1. 🚦 Can you make your traffic light change speed based on the LDR reading? *(brighter room = faster cycle)*
2. 💡 Find **3 places** in your home where something turns on/off automatically. What is the sensor? What is the action?
3. 🔥 **Bonus challenge:** Ask AI *"How do I add a button to my traffic light that keeps green on longer when pressed?"* — try it in Wokwi first!

:::

# AI Onboarding — Your Lab Partner {.sdaia-dark background-gradient="linear-gradient(135deg, #1C355E, #00C9A7)"}

## 4 Ways to Use AI in This Course

In this course, AI is like a **knowledgeable friend** — it can help you think, but you still need to test the robot yourself.

1. **Explain** — *"What does `pinMode(13, OUTPUT)` actually do? Explain it simply."*
2. **Debug** — *"My LED won't light up. Here is my code. What might be wrong?"*
3. **Extend** — *"My traffic light works. How do I make the yellow light blink slowly?"*
4. **Generate** — *"Write code to set up 3 LED pins on an Arduino Uno."*

::: {.callout-warning}

## Always Test It!
AI can make mistakes. **Never trust AI code without uploading it and seeing it run.**

:::

**Setup:** Open Claude or ChatGPT · Log in to your assigned account · First prompt: *"I'm learning Arduino. Can you explain what `digitalWrite` does using a simple analogy?"*

## Questions? {.sdaia-dark background-gradient="linear-gradient(135deg, #1C355E, #00C9A7)"}

::: {.r-fit-text}

🙋

:::
